chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Performed on Behalf of the Deceased Regarding the Pilgrimage of Islam, Whether They Made a Will or Not

text: 2919 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَارِثِ بْنِ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ اِبْنَتِي أَوْصَتْ بِحَجَّةٍ وَ لَمْ تَحُجَّ قَالَ «فَحُجَّ عَنْهَا فَإِنَّهَا لَكَ وَ لَهَا» قُلْتُ إِنَّ أُمِّي مَاتَتْ وَ لَمْ تَحُجَّ قَالَ «حُجَّ عَنْهَا فَإِنَّهَا لَكَ وَ لَهَا ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2919 - It was narrated from Al-Harith ibn Al-Mughira, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "My daughter instructed in her will for a Hajj to be performed, but she did not perform Hajj during her lifetime."
Imam (as) said: "Perform Hajj on her behalf; the reward will be for both you and her."
I said: "My mother passed away and did not perform Hajj."
Imam (as) said: "Perform Hajj on her behalf; the reward will be for both you and her."
